attack_surface.data_description: Signed claim transaction with recipient address and allocation proof/identifier.

security_gap.gap_description: The system may not adequately verify the claimant's right to the allocation.

actor_risk.description: An external user attempting to claim assets without proper authorization.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Exploiting this flaw leads to unauthorized asset transfer.

assumptions: The airdrop claim contract or off-chain service fails to adequately verify the user's entitlement to claim the specific allocation.

mitigation.strategy: Implement strong authentication and authorization checks based on cryptographically verifiable proof of allocation ownership.

threats.description: An attacker impersonates a legitimate user to claim their BERA airdrop allocation.

origin.capability_match: Initiate transaction, Sign transaction, Approve ERC20

attack_surface.data_description: Signed swap transaction including trade pair, amounts, slippage, recipient.

security_gap.gap_description: Logic vulnerability in BEX smart contract allows unauthorized actions or asset manipulation.

actor_risk.description: An external user interacting with the core exchange contract with potential to exploit vulnerabilities.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Exploiting BEX contract vulnerabilities can lead to significant asset loss and market disruption.

assumptions: The BEX smart contract contains an exploitable logic vulnerability (e.g., re-entrancy, incorrect accounting, access control flaw).

mitigation.strategy: Conduct comprehensive smart contract audits and formal verification.

threats.description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the BEX smart contract to steal funds from liquidity pools, execute unauthorized trades, or manipulate pool parameters.

trace_source.matched_capability: Initiate transaction, Sign transaction, Approve ERC20

origin.capability_match: Initiate transaction, Sign transaction, Provide proof

attack_surface.data_description: Signed claim transaction including proof data (e.g., Merkle proof), claimant address, and claim details.

security_gap.gap_description: Flaw in off-chain proof generation/distribution or on-chain validation allows claiming unearned or other users' rewards.

actor_risk.description: An external user attempting to claim rewards without proper authorization.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Exploiting the claiming mechanism directly leads to theft of user funds.

assumptions: The off-chain incentive proof service generates flawed proofs, proofs can be tampered with, or the on-chain contract validates proofs incorrectly allowing unauthorized claims.
An attacker can obtain or fabricate a valid proof for rewards they are not entitled to.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure secure off-chain proof generation and distribution, implement robust on-chain proof validation logic strictly tied to the claimant's identity.

threats.description: An attacker obtains or fabricates a malicious proof to claim incentive tokens or BGT rewards belonging to other users or claim more tokens than earned.

trace_source.matched_capability: Initiate transaction, Sign transaction, Provide proof

origin.capability_match: Initiate transaction, Sign transaction, Define proposal

attack_surface.data_description: Signed transaction to create a proposal with title, description, and encoded call data.

security_gap.gap_description: Governance contract susceptible to spam or resource exhaustion via proposal creation.

actor_risk.description: An external user with sufficient BGT to create proposals.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Disrupts the governance process but doesn't directly lead to asset loss (unless governance controls are exploitable).

assumptions: The governance contract does not adequately rate-limit proposals or handle malicious proposal content efficiently.
The cost of creating spam proposals is low relative to the disruption caused.

mitigation.strategy: Implement rate limiting on proposal creation per address and ensure efficient contract handling of proposal data.

threats.description: An attacker creates a large number of spam or resource-intensive proposals to disrupt the governance process, preventing legitimate proposals or votes.

trace_source.matched_capability: Initiate transaction, Sign transaction, Define proposal

origin.capability_match: Initiate transaction, Sign transaction, Approve ERC20, Set incentive rate

attack_surface.data_description: Signed transaction including target vault, incentive token address, amount, and rate.

security_gap.gap_description: Weak access control allows non-managers to add incentives or managers to add unapproved tokens/rates.

actor_risk.description: An external user who may not be the designated Token Manager attempting to add incentives.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Unauthorized adding of incentives disrupts validator economics and can lead to financial loss.

assumptions: The Reward Vault Management contract does not correctly verify that the transaction initiator is the designated Token Manager for the specified vault/token.
The mapping of authorized managers to tokens/vaults is incorrect or vulnerable to manipulation.

mitigation.strategy: Implement strict role-based access control in the contract, ensuring only authorized Token Managers can add incentives for their designated tokens/vaults.

threats.description: An attacker impersonates a Token Manager or exploits an access control vulnerability to add malicious or misconfigured incentive tokens to a Reward Vault, disrupting validator rewards and harming legitimate users.

trace_source.matched_capability: Initiate transaction, Sign transaction, Approve ERC20, Set incentive rate

origin.capability_match: Initiate transaction, Sign transaction, Approve ERC20

attack_surface.data_description: Signed mint transaction including collateral type, amount, and desired HONEY amount.

security_gap.gap_description: Logic vulnerability in HoneySwap contract or associated vaults allows minting HONEY without sufficient/valid collateral or at incorrect rates.

actor_risk.description: An external user interacting with the stablecoin minting contract with potential to exploit vulnerabilities.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Exploiting minting contract vulnerabilities can lead to system insolvency and loss of user collateral.

assumptions: The HoneySwap or Vault smart contracts contain exploitable logic (e.g., incorrect calculation of minted HONEY, re-entrancy with collateral tokens).
The system relies on an insecure price feed for collateral valuation (if applicable).

mitigation.strategy: Conduct comprehensive smart contract audits and formal verification, ensure secure collateral handling and minting calculations.

threats.description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the HoneySwap minting contract to mint HONEY without providing adequate collateral or manipulate minting/burning rates, potentially causing bad debt or instability.

trace_source.matched_capability: Initiate transaction, Sign transaction, Approve ERC20

attack_surface.data_description: Data displayed to the user about validators, vaults, portfolio, etc.

security_gap.gap_description: The off-chain service or UI serving public data is compromised, allowing data to be tampered with before presentation.

actor_risk.description: An external user consuming data from off-chain services.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Misleading users can cause them to make incorrect decisions or interact with malicious contracts.

assumptions: The off-chain service (UI, API) serving public data is vulnerable to compromise or data manipulation.
The communication channel between the service and the user is not secured.

mitigation.strategy: Implement secure coding practices for services, use HTTPS, and advise users to verify critical information on-chain explorer.

threats.description: An attacker compromises the BeraHub/BEX UI or backend service to display misleading information (e.g., false APRs, incorrect claimable amounts), potentially tricking users into harmful actions.

attack_surface.data_description: UI interaction, data transmission

security_gap.gap_description: Lack of strong authentication or session validation allows impersonation at the application layer.

actor_risk.description: An external user attempting to impersonate another user to perform actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful impersonation allows unauthorized delegation of BGT, a critical asset, affecting staking rewards.

assumptions: The application interface (Hub) does not sufficiently verify the user's identity for the boost action.
An attacker can compromise a user's session or identity token.

mitigation.strategy: Implement multi-factor authentication and strong session management.

threats.description: An attacker impersonates a legitimate user via the Berachain Hub to delegate their BGT to an unintended validator.

attack_surface.data_description: UI interaction, data transmission

security_gap.gap_description: Data transmitted between the user and the system is not protected against modification.

actor_risk.description: An attacker intercepts and modifies data in transit.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Modification of boost parameters can redirect BGT delegation, impacting user rewards and validator economics.

assumptions: The communication channel (e.g., HTTP) between the user's wallet/browser and the Hub is not encrypted or lacks integrity checks.
The Hub or Validator does not validate the integrity of received boost parameters.

mitigation.strategy: Implement HTTPS/WSS for all communications and require digitally signed boost requests.

threats.description: An attacker intercepts and modifies the boost request initiated by a user, changing the target validator or the amount of BGT delegated.

attack_surface.data_description: UI interaction, data transmission

security_gap.gap_description: System endpoints are vulnerable to request flooding.

actor_risk.description: An attacker attempts to overload system resources to prevent service availability.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Disrupting the ability to boost validators can impact user participation and the Proof-of-Liquidity mechanism.

assumptions: Berachain Hub or Validator endpoints lack sufficient rate limiting, request filtering, or capacity to handle request floods.
Processing boost requests is resource-intensive.

mitigation.strategy: Implement rate limiting, DDoS protection, and scale infrastructure for request handling.

threats.description: An attacker floods the Berachain Hub or Validator endpoint with boost requests, preventing legitimate users from boosting validators.

attack_surface.data_description: UI interaction, data transmission

security_gap.gap_description: Lack of strong authentication or proof validation allows unauthorized claim initiation.

actor_risk.description: An external user attempting to impersonate another user to claim assets.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful impersonation leads to theft of user's earned incentive tokens.

assumptions: The application interface (Hub) or the claim process does not sufficiently verify the user's identity or ownership of the claim entitlement.
An attacker can obtain or forge a user's claim proof.

mitigation.strategy: Implement strong authentication and require cryptographically secure, user-signed claim proofs validated on-chain.

threats.description: An attacker impersonates a legitimate user via the Berachain Hub to claim the user's earned incentive tokens from the BGTIncentiveDistributor.

attack_surface.data_description: UI interaction, data transmission

security_gap.gap_description: Data transmitted between the user and the system or proof data is not protected against modification.

actor_risk.description: An attacker intercepts and modifies data or proof in transit.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Modification of claim requests or proofs can redirect incentive tokens to an attacker or allow claiming beyond entitlement.

assumptions: The communication channel (e.g., HTTP) is not encrypted or lacks integrity checks.
Claim proof data can be modified in transit and still be accepted.
The BGTIncentiveDistributor contract does not sufficiently validate the integrity of the claim request or proof.

mitigation.strategy: Implement HTTPS/WSS, use authenticated and integrity-protected claim proofs, and validate proof integrity on-chain.

threats.description: An attacker intercepts and modifies the claim request or the associated proof to steal a user's incentive tokens or claim excess amounts.

attack_surface.data_description: UI interaction, data transmission

security_gap.gap_description: Sensitive data like claim proofs or entitlement details are exposed during transmission.

actor_risk.description: An attacker intercepts sensitive user data.

behavioral_risk.rationale: While sensitive, the direct impact is privacy violation rather than asset loss unless combined with other attacks.

assumptions: The communication channel (e.g., HTTP) is not encrypted.
Claim proofs or entitlement details are transmitted in plain text or easily decipherable formats.

mitigation.strategy: Enforce HTTPS/WSS for all communications.

threats.description: An attacker intercepts claim proofs or details about a user's earned incentives during transit between the user and the Berachain Hub.

attack_surface.data_description: UI interaction, data transmission

security_gap.gap_description: System endpoints are vulnerable to request flooding.

actor_risk.description: An attacker attempts to overload system resources to prevent service availability.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Preventing users from claiming incentives disrupts the token flow and economic participation in the network.

assumptions: Berachain Hub or BGTIncentiveDistributor endpoints lack sufficient rate limiting, request filtering, or capacity.
Processing claim requests is resource-intensive.

mitigation.strategy: Implement rate limiting, DDoS protection, and scale infrastructure for request handling.

threats.description: An attacker floods the Berachain Hub or BGTIncentiveDistributor contract with claim requests, preventing legitimate users from claiming their earned incentives.

attack_surface.data_description: Claim request

security_gap.gap_description: BGTIncentiveDistributor contract incorrectly validates claim proofs or authorization, allowing unauthorized claims.

actor_risk.description: An attacker attempts to gain unauthorized privileges to access or control other users' assets.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Exploiting contract validation can lead to theft of assets belonging to other users.

assumptions: The BGTIncentiveDistributor contract has a vulnerability in its claim proof validation logic.
An attacker can craft a malicious proof that passes the contract's validation.
The proof generation mechanism is predictable or exploitable.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure robust, audited claim proof validation logic in the BGTIncentiveDistributor contract.

threats.description: An attacker crafts a malicious claim proof to exploit a vulnerability in the BGTIncentiveDistributor contract, allowing them to claim incentive tokens belonging to other users.

attack_surface.data_description: Swap transaction parameters (input/output token, amounts, recipient)

security_gap.gap_description: Transaction parameters can be altered via a compromised interface before signing.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with a dApp interface (e.g., BeraHub) to initiate a swap.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Reliance on the integrity of the dApp interface for critical transaction details.

assumptions: User interacts with a potentially compromised BeraHub or dApp interface.
Wallet does not provide clear, itemized breakdown of signed transaction details.
Attacker controls the interface presented to the user.

mitigation.strategy: Implement strong interface security measures.

threats.description: An attacker tampers with swap parameters (e.g., recipient address) via a compromised interface, redirecting funds.

attack_surface.data_description: Liquidity deposit/withdrawal transaction parameters

security_gap.gap_description: Excessive transactions can congest contract processing or increase gas costs for others.

actor_risk.description: User sends a high volume of small transactions to the liquidity pool.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Potential for minor network or contract congestion and increased transaction costs.

assumptions: Transaction fees are low enough to make spamming economically feasible for an attacker.
The BEX contract processing is sensitive to transaction volume or frequency.
Network capacity is limited.

mitigation.strategy: Implement transaction rate limiting or dynamic fees.

threats.description: An attacker spams small liquidity deposit and withdrawal operations on BEX, causing congestion.

attack_surface.data_description: Claim transaction details (amount, source vault)

security_gap.gap_description: Transaction data is publicly visible on the blockchain explorer.

actor_risk.description: User participates in BGT earning activities.

behavioral_risk.rationale: This is an inherent characteristic of a public blockchain.

assumptions: User desires privacy regarding their specific BGT earning sources and amounts.
Blockchain explorers make this on-chain data easily accessible.

mitigation.strategy: Educate users about blockchain transparency.

threats.description: User's BGT claiming activity, including amount and source Reward Vault, is publicly visible on-chain.

attack_surface.data_description: BGT delegation transaction details (amount, target validator)

security_gap.gap_description: Transaction data is publicly visible on the blockchain explorer.

actor_risk.description: User delegates BGT to validators.

behavioral_risk.rationale: This is an inherent characteristic of a public blockchain.

assumptions: User desires privacy for their BGT delegation strategy and amounts.
Blockchain explorers make this on-chain data easily accessible.

mitigation.strategy: Educate users about blockchain transparency.

threats.description: User's BGT delegation activity, including amount and target validator, is publicly visible on-chain.

attack_surface.data_description: Incentive claim proof (e.g., Merkle proof) and associated claim details

security_gap.gap_description: Claim relies on proofs generated by a potentially compromised off-chain backend service.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with the incentive claiming mechanism.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Compromise of the off-chain proof generation system could lead to theft of user assets (Incentive Tokens).

assumptions: The backend service responsible for generating incentive claim proofs is compromised.
The on-chain verification relies on the integrity of the provided proof (e.g., Merkle proof).
An attacker can generate valid-looking proofs for claims they are not entitled to.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust security controls for the backend proof generation service.

threats.description: An attacker compromises the proof generation backend to forge claim proofs and steal other users' incentive tokens.

attack_surface.data_description: Rapid BGT delegation/undelegation transactions

security_gap.gap_description: Rapid changes in delegation disrupt voting power calculations and potentially governance processes.

actor_risk.description: User controls a significant amount of BGT.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Potential to disrupt the stability and predictability of governance voting power.

assumptions: Attacker controls a substantial amount of BGT.
The system allows rapid delegation changes without significant penalty (e.g., cooldowns, high fees).
Governance processes are sensitive to sudden shifts in total delegated BGT or voting power distribution.

mitigation.strategy: Implement cooldown periods or increased costs for frequent BGT delegation changes.

threats.description: An attacker with significant BGT disrupts governance by rapidly changing their delegation across validators.

attack_surface.data_description: Redemption transaction parameters (BGT amount, BERA recipient address)

security_gap.gap_description: Transaction parameters can be altered via a compromised interface before signing.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with the redemption interface (e.g., BeraHub).

behavioral_risk.rationale: Reliance on the integrity of the dApp interface for a high-value operation.

assumptions: User interacts with a potentially compromised BeraHub interface.
Wallet does not provide clear, itemized breakdown of signed transaction details.
Attacker controls the interface presented to the user.

mitigation.strategy: Implement strong interface security measures.

threats.description: An attacker tampers with BGT redemption parameters (e.g., BERA recipient address) via a compromised interface.

attack_surface.data_description: proof data, signature

security_gap.gap_description: The proof data required to claim incentives might be forgeable or replayable.

actor_risk.description: An external user interacting with the claim mechanism.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Compromised proof integrity allows unauthorized claiming of incentive tokens, leading to asset loss.

assumptions: The backend service generating proofs does not sufficiently secure or sign the proof data.
The BGTIncentiveDistributor contract does not fully validate the authenticity or non-replayability of the submitted proof.

mitigation.strategy: Implement cryptographic signing of claim proofs by a trusted off-chain service, and verify signatures on-chain.

threats.description: An attacker may spoof or replay claim proof data, potentially allowing them to claim incentive tokens that belong to other users or claim tokens multiple times.

attack_surface.data_description: proof data, signature

security_gap.gap_description: Proof data or associated claims may be tampered with before submission to the contract.

actor_risk.description: An external user interacting with the claim mechanism.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering with the claim proof could redirect incentives or inflate claim amounts, leading to unauthorized asset transfer.

assumptions: The claim proof data format or validation is susceptible to modification.
The link between the proof and the specific claim amount/recipient is not fully secured.

mitigation.strategy: Hash or sign the proof data and the specific claim details together; validate the composite hash/signature on-chain.

threats.description: An attacker may tamper with the proof data or the claim request before submitting it to the BGTIncentiveDistributor contract, potentially altering the recipient or amount of incentive tokens claimed.

attack_surface.data_description: Validator ID, amount of BGT to delegate

security_gap.gap_description: Delegation request parameters are not fully tamper-proof or validated against user intent.

actor_risk.description: The actor is a standard user interacting with the validator.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Modification of delegation amounts or targets could disrupt validator ranking and reward distribution.

assumptions: The delegation request allows modification after signing or before validation.
The validator node or contract does not fully validate all input parameters against user context.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure all delegation request parameters are cryptographically bound to the user's signature and strictly validated.

threats.description: An attacker tampers with the delegation request to change the amount of BGT delegated or the target validator.

attack_surface.data_description: Validator ID, amount of BGT to delegate

security_gap.gap_description: The delegation action is not sufficiently logged or cryptographically signed to prevent denial.

actor_risk.description: The actor is a standard user interacting with the validator.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Blockchain transactions are generally non-repudiable if properly signed and recorded.

assumptions: The delegation transaction might not be permanently recorded or cryptographically linked to the user in a way that prevents denial.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure all delegation transactions are recorded on-chain with user signatures.

threats.description: An actor denies having delegated BGT to a specific validator.

attack_surface.data_description: Validator ID, amount of BGT to delegate

security_gap.gap_description: Insufficient rate limiting or resource protection on the validator node or network against excessive requests.

actor_risk.description: The actor is a standard user interacting with the validator.

behavioral_risk.rationale: A high volume of delegation requests could degrade validator performance or network availability.

assumptions: The validator node or network infrastructure lacks sufficient rate limiting.
Processing delegation requests consumes significant validator resources.

mitigation.strategy: Implement rate limiting and resource protection measures on validator nodes and network infrastructure.

threats.description: An attacker floods the validator with delegation requests, potentially disrupting its operation or the network.

attack_surface.data_description: Claim proof, token amounts, recipient address

security_gap.gap_description: Weak proof generation or validation allows an attacker to forge claims.

actor_risk.description: The actor is a standard user interacting with the distributor.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful spoofing can lead to the theft of incentive tokens belonging to other users.

assumptions: The cryptographic proof required for claiming can be forged or replayed.
The claim validation process in the contract or off-chain service is flawed.

mitigation.strategy: Strengthen claim proof generation and validation to prevent forgery and replay attacks.

threats.description: An attacker forges a claim proof to steal incentive tokens belonging to another user by impersonating them.

attack_surface.data_description: Claim proof, token amounts, recipient address

security_gap.gap_description: Claim request parameters are not fully covered by the proof/signature or validated against the user's entitlement.

actor_risk.description: The actor is a standard user interacting with the distributor.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering could lead to the theft of incentive tokens or inflation of claimed amounts.

assumptions: The claim proof does not cryptographically bind all relevant claim parameters (e.g., amount, recipient).
The contract or off-chain service does not fully validate all parameters against the proof or entitlement.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure the claim proof cryptographically covers all critical claim parameters and implement strict validation on-chain.

threats.description: An attacker modifies the claim request to change the amount of incentive tokens claimed or the recipient address.

attack_surface.data_description: Claim proof, token amounts, recipient address

security_gap.gap_description: The claim action is not sufficiently logged or cryptographically signed to prevent denial.

actor_risk.description: The actor is a standard user interacting with the distributor.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Blockchain transactions are generally non-repudiable if properly signed and recorded.

assumptions: The claim transaction might not be permanently recorded or cryptographically linked to the user in a way that prevents denial.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure all claim transactions are recorded on-chain with user signatures.

threats.description: An actor denies having claimed incentive tokens.

attack_surface.data_description: Claim proof, token amounts, recipient address

security_gap.gap_description: Insufficient rate limiting or resource protection on the distributor contract/API against excessive requests.

actor_risk.description: The actor is a standard user interacting with the distributor.

behavioral_risk.rationale: A high volume of claim requests could disrupt the claiming process for legitimate users.

assumptions: The distributor contract or its interface lacks sufficient rate limiting for claim submissions.
Processing claim requests consumes significant resources.

mitigation.strategy: Implement rate limiting for claim submissions and optimize claim processing logic.

threats.description: An attacker floods the incentive distributor with claim requests, potentially disrupting the claiming process for others.

attack_surface.data_description: Proposal ID, vote option, voting power

security_gap.gap_description: Lack of proper authentication/signature verification for voting allows impersonation.

actor_risk.description: The actor is a standard user interacting with the governance contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Spoofed votes can manipulate governance outcomes, impacting the entire network.

assumptions: The governance contract or voting interface does not require cryptographic signatures for votes.
An attacker can submit votes on behalf of other BGT holders or their delegates.

mitigation.strategy: Require cryptographic signatures for all votes and verify voter identity/delegated power.

threats.description: An attacker impersonates a legitimate voter to cast fraudulent votes, influencing governance decisions.

attack_surface.data_description: Proposal ID, vote option, voting power

security_gap.gap_description: Vote parameters are not tamper-proof after submission.

actor_risk.description: The actor is a standard user interacting with the governance contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering with votes can alter governance outcomes.

assumptions: Vote data is mutable after submission.
The vote's integrity is not cryptographically verified against potential modifications.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure vote data is immutable after recording and use cryptographic signatures that cover all parameters.

threats.description: An attacker modifies a vote after it has been cast, potentially changing the outcome of a governance proposal.

attack_surface.data_description: Proposal ID, vote option, voting power

security_gap.gap_description: The vote action is not sufficiently logged or cryptographically signed to prevent denial.

actor_risk.description: The actor is a standard user interacting with the governance contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Blockchain records mitigate repudiation risks for on-chain actions.

assumptions: The voting record might not be permanently stored on-chain or linked cryptographically to the user in a way that prevents denial.

mitigation.strategy: Record all votes on-chain with associated voter signatures.

threats.description: An actor denies having cast a specific vote on a governance proposal.

attack_surface.data_description: Proposal ID, vote option, voting power

security_gap.gap_description: Insufficient rate limiting or resource protection on the governance contract/API against excessive submissions.

actor_risk.description: The actor is a standard user interacting with the governance contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Spamming vote submissions could prevent legitimate users from participating or disrupt vote tallying.

assumptions: The governance contract or its interface lacks sufficient rate limiting for vote submissions.
Processing vote submissions consumes significant resources.

mitigation.strategy: Implement rate limiting for vote submissions and optimize vote processing.

threats.description: An attacker floods the governance system with vote submissions, potentially preventing legitimate users from voting or disrupting the tallying process.

attack_surface.data_description: Vault ID, asset type, amount to stake

security_gap.gap_description: Staking request parameters are not fully tamper-proof or validated against user intent.

actor_risk.description: The actor is a standard user interacting with the reward vault.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering could lead to staking incorrect assets/amounts or into the wrong vault, potentially impacting user funds and BGT rewards.

assumptions: The staking request allows modification of parameters after signing or before validation.
The reward vault contract does not fully validate all input parameters against user intent or asset ownership.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure all staking request parameters are cryptographically bound to the user's signature and strictly validated on-chain.

threats.description: An attacker modifies a staking request to stake a different amount or asset than intended, or stake into a different vault.

attack_surface.data_description: Vault ID, asset type, amount to stake

security_gap.gap_description: The staking action is not sufficiently logged or cryptographically signed to prevent denial.

actor_risk.description: The actor is a standard user interacting with the reward vault.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Blockchain transactions are generally non-repudiable if properly signed and recorded.

assumptions: The staking transaction might not be permanently recorded or cryptographically linked to the user in a way that prevents denial.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure all staking transactions are recorded on-chain with user signatures.

threats.description: An actor denies having staked assets in a specific reward vault.

attack_surface.data_description: Vault ID, asset type, amount to stake

security_gap.gap_description: Insufficient rate limiting or resource protection on the reward vault contract/API against excessive requests.

actor_risk.description: The actor is a standard user interacting with the reward vault.

behavioral_risk.rationale: A high volume of staking requests could clog the contract or network, preventing others from staking.

assumptions: The reward vault contract or its interface lacks sufficient rate limiting for staking submissions.
Processing staking requests consumes significant resources.

mitigation.strategy: Implement rate limiting for staking requests and optimize staking logic.

threats.description: An attacker floods the reward vault with staking requests, potentially disrupting the staking process for legitimate users.

attack_surface.data_description: Asset pair, amounts to provide

security_gap.gap_description: Liquidity provision parameters are not fully tamper-proof or validated against user intent.

actor_risk.description: The actor is a standard user interacting with the BEX contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering could lead to loss of user funds by providing incorrect assets/amounts or interacting with the wrong pool.

assumptions: The liquidity provision data allows modification after signing or before validation.
The BEX contract does not fully validate all input parameters against user intent or asset ownership.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure liquidity provision data is cryptographically bound to the user's signature and strictly validated on-chain.

threats.description: An attacker modifies the liquidity provision data to provide different assets or amounts than intended, or provide liquidity to a different pool.

attack_surface.data_description: Asset pair, amounts to provide

security_gap.gap_description: The liquidity provision action is not sufficiently logged or cryptographically signed to prevent denial.

actor_risk.description: The actor is a standard user interacting with the BEX contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Blockchain transactions are generally non-repudiable if properly signed and recorded.

assumptions: The liquidity provision transaction might not be permanently recorded or cryptographically linked to the user in a way that prevents denial.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure all liquidity provision transactions are recorded on-chain with user signatures.

threats.description: An actor denies having provided liquidity to a specific pool on BEX.

attack_surface.data_description: Asset pair, amounts to provide

security_gap.gap_description: Insufficient rate limiting or resource protection on the BEX contract/API against excessive requests.

actor_risk.description: The actor is a standard user interacting with the BEX contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: A high volume of liquidity provision requests could clog the contract or network, impacting trading or other liquidity providers.

assumptions: The BEX contract or its interface lacks sufficient rate limiting for liquidity provision submissions.
Processing liquidity provision requests consumes significant resources.

mitigation.strategy: Implement rate limiting for liquidity provision requests and optimize contract logic.

threats.description: An attacker floods the BEX with liquidity provision requests, potentially disrupting the exchange or specific pools.

attack_surface.data_description: Claim amount, recipient address

security_gap.gap_description: Lack of proper authentication/signature verification for claiming BGT rewards allows impersonation.

actor_risk.description: The actor is a standard user interacting with the reward vault.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful spoofing can lead to the theft of BGT rewards belonging to other users.

assumptions: The reward vault contract or claiming interface does not require cryptographic signatures for claims.
An attacker can submit BGT claims on behalf of other stakers.

mitigation.strategy: Require cryptographic signatures for all BGT claim requests and verify claimant identity/entitlement.

threats.description: An attacker impersonates another user to claim their accumulated BGT rewards from a reward vault.

attack_surface.data_description: Claim amount, recipient address

security_gap.gap_description: Claim request parameters are not fully tamper-proof or validated against user entitlement.

actor_risk.description: The actor is a standard user interacting with the reward vault.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering could lead to theft of BGT rewards or inflation of claimed amounts.

assumptions: The BGT claim request allows modification of parameters after signing or before validation.
The contract does not validate claim parameters against accumulated rewards or intended recipient.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure all BGT claim request parameters are cryptographically bound to the user's signature and strictly validated.

threats.description: An attacker modifies a BGT claim request to change the amount of BGT claimed or the recipient address.

attack_surface.data_description: Claim amount, recipient address

security_gap.gap_description: The claim action is not sufficiently logged or cryptographically signed to prevent denial.

actor_risk.description: The actor is a standard user interacting with the reward vault.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Blockchain transactions are generally non-repudiable if properly signed and recorded.

assumptions: The BGT claim transaction might not be permanently recorded or cryptographically linked to the user in a way that prevents denial.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure all BGT claim transactions are recorded on-chain with user signatures.

threats.description: An actor denies having claimed BGT rewards from a specific reward vault.

attack_surface.data_description: Claim amount, recipient address

security_gap.gap_description: Insufficient rate limiting or resource protection on the reward vault contract/API against excessive claim requests.

actor_risk.description: The actor is a standard user interacting with the reward vault.

behavioral_risk.rationale: A high volume of claim requests could clog the contract or network, preventing others from claiming rewards.

assumptions: The reward vault contract or its interface lacks sufficient rate limiting for claims.
Processing BGT claim requests consumes significant resources.

mitigation.strategy: Implement rate limiting for claim requests and optimize contract logic.

threats.description: An attacker floods the reward vault with BGT claim requests, potentially disrupting the process for legitimate users.

attack_surface.data_description: Claim request (proof + TX signature)

security_gap.gap_description: Airdrop eligibility proof may be forged or misused, allowing unauthorized claims.

actor_risk.description: An external actor attempts to claim BERA tokens they are not entitled to.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful spoofing leads to unauthorized acquisition of the native asset ($BERA).

assumptions: The mechanism for verifying airdrop eligibility (e.g., NFT ownership, social link proof) has a vulnerability.
An attacker can obtain, forge, or reuse eligibility proofs belonging to others.

mitigation.strategy: Strengthen eligibility verification logic; implement unique, non-reusable claim proofs.

threats.description: An attacker may impersonate an eligible user or exploit a vulnerability in the claim process to claim BERA airdrop tokens allocated to others.

attack_surface.data_description: Swap parameters + TX signature

security_gap.gap_description: DEX smart contract may not sufficiently validate swap parameters (e.g., slippage) or protect against flash loan attacks.

actor_risk.description: An external actor attempts to manipulate swap execution or outcomes.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful tampering can lead to unfair swap prices, asset loss for users, or liquidity pool drain.

assumptions: The BEX smart contract has a vulnerability related to swap execution, pricing, or parameter validation.
An attacker can craft a transaction to exploit this vulnerability.

mitigation.strategy: Implement stringent slippage checks, utilize robust price oracles, and apply reentrancy guards.

threats.description: An attacker may exploit vulnerabilities in the BEX smart contract to tamper with swap parameters or execution, leading to manipulated prices or unauthorized access to liquidity.

attack_surface.data_description: Swap parameters + TX signature

security_gap.gap_description: High transaction volume or large swaps can exhaust liquidity or congest the BEX contract, preventing legitimate users from trading.

actor_risk.description: An external actor attempts to disrupt BEX operations through excessive transactions or liquidity manipulation.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful DoS impairs the core functionality of the BEX for all users.

assumptions: The BEX smart contract or underlying blockchain infrastructure is susceptible to resource exhaustion from high transaction volume or specific swap patterns.
An attacker has sufficient resources (e.g., capital for large swaps, BERA for gas) to execute a DoS attack.

mitigation.strategy: Implement transaction rate limits or dynamic fees; ensure sufficient liquidity and network capacity.

threats.description: An attacker could flood the BEX with transactions or execute large, strategic swaps to deplete liquidity pools or congest the network, causing a denial of service for other users.

attack_surface.data_description: Mint/Redeem parameters + TX signature

security_gap.gap_description: HoneySwap vault or factory smart contract may allow manipulation of minting/redemption amounts or bypass collateral requirements.

actor_risk.description: An external actor attempts to mint HONEY without sufficient collateral or manipulate redemption amounts.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful tampering leads to unauthorized HONEY creation or theft of deposited collateral.

assumptions: The HoneySwap vault or factory smart contract contains a vulnerability in its minting or redemption logic.
An attacker can craft a transaction to exploit this vulnerability.

mitigation.strategy: Rigorously validate mint/redeem parameters against collateral amounts and rates; perform extensive smart contract audits.

threats.description: An attacker could exploit vulnerabilities in the HoneySwap smart contracts to tamper with the minting or redemption process, potentially minting unauthorized HONEY or stealing collateral.

attack_surface.data_description: Liquidity pool parameters + TX signature

security_gap.gap_description: BEX smart contract may allow manipulating liquidity provision parameters or calculations, leading to incorrect LP token issuance or asset distribution.

actor_risk.description: An external actor attempts to manipulate the liquidity provision process for personal gain.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful tampering could result in unfair distribution of LP tokens or affect pool integrity.

assumptions: The BEX smart contract has a vulnerability in the logic for adding liquidity and calculating LP tokens.
An attacker can submit crafted parameters to exploit this vulnerability.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust validation and auditing for liquidity addition calculations and LP token issuance.

threats.description: An attacker could tamper with the liquidity provision process on BEX by exploiting flaws in the smart contract logic, potentially receiving an unfair amount of LP tokens or disrupting the pool's balance.

attack_surface.data_description: LP token amount + TX signature

security_gap.gap_description: BEX smart contract may not sufficiently verify ownership or validity of LP tokens during withdrawal.

actor_risk.description: An external actor attempts to withdraw liquidity using LP tokens they do not legitimately own.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful spoofing allows unauthorized removal of assets from liquidity pools.

assumptions: The BEX smart contract has a vulnerability allowing withdrawal with invalid or stolen LP token proofs.
An attacker can acquire or forge proofs of LP token ownership for tokens they don't own.

mitigation.strategy: Enforce strict verification of LP token ownership via standard ERC-20 mechanisms within the withdrawal logic.

threats.description: An attacker could potentially impersonate a legitimate liquidity provider by spoofing LP token ownership to withdraw assets from BEX liquidity pools they are not entitled to.

attack_surface.data_description: LP token amount + TX signature

security_gap.gap_description: BEX smart contract may allow manipulation of withdrawal calculations or asset distribution based on LP tokens.

actor_risk.description: An external actor attempts to manipulate liquidity withdrawal amounts or asset distribution.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful tampering can lead to over-withdrawal of assets or unfair distribution from pools.

assumptions: The BEX smart contract has a vulnerability in the logic for withdrawing liquidity and calculating asset returns based on LP tokens.
An attacker can submit crafted parameters to exploit this vulnerability.

mitigation.strategy: Rigorously validate withdrawal amounts against LP token balance and apply accurate calculations for asset returns.

threats.description: An attacker could tamper with the liquidity withdrawal process on BEX by exploiting flaws in the smart contract logic, potentially withdrawing more assets than their LP tokens entitle them to.

attack_surface.data_description: Validator ID, BGT amount, transaction data

security_gap.gap_description: Request origin is not verified, allowing spoofing.

actor_risk.description: Unauthenticated external user sending requests.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Requests may lack proper authentication or signature verification, critical for financial actions.

assumptions: Attacker can forge requests appearing to come from a user.
Hub endpoint or smart contract does not verify sender identity cryptographically.

mitigation.strategy: Implement request signing and verification on sensitive actions.

threats.description: An attacker sends a boost request appearing to originate from another user, potentially causing incorrect validator boosts or BGT transfers.

attack_surface.data_description: Validator ID, BGT amount, transaction data

security_gap.gap_description: Request payload can be altered in transit.

actor_risk.description: User or attacker manipulating transaction data.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Request payload might not be integrity-protected (signed over content), allowing manipulation of amount or target.

assumptions: Attacker can intercept and modify the request payload.
Hub endpoint or smart contract accepts modified payload without detecting tampering.

mitigation.strategy: Implement request signing covering the entire payload hash.

threats.description: An attacker intercepts a boost request and modifies the validator ID or BGT amount before it is processed.

attack_surface.data_description: Validator ID, BGT amount, transaction data

security_gap.gap_description: Authorization check fails to ensure action is on behalf of authenticated user with ownership.

actor_risk.description: Attacker gains ability to act as another user regarding BGT boosting.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Weak authorization allows unauthorized cross-user BGT actions.

assumptions: Authentication mechanism is weak (e.g., session hijacking).
Request processing does not verify BGT ownership against the authenticated user ID before action.

mitigation.strategy: Enforce strong authentication and verify BGT ownership against authenticated user ID for boost actions.

threats.description: An attacker exploits weak authorization or authentication to queue or activate a BGT boost using another user's BGT balance.

attack_surface.data_description: Proposal ID, vote choice, signature

security_gap.gap_description: Vote origin or signature verification is weak.

actor_risk.description: Attacker sending fraudulent votes.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Votes determine governance outcomes; lack of strong cryptographic verification is critical.

assumptions: Governance contract doesn't verify the signature against the BGT holder's address.
Attacker can generate signatures for arbitrary addresses.

mitigation.strategy: Governance contract must cryptographically verify vote signatures against BGT holder addresses with sufficient voting power.

threats.description: An attacker submits a vote to the governance contract using a forged signature or identity, potentially influencing proposal outcomes.

attack_surface.data_description: Proposal ID, vote choice, signature

security_gap.gap_description: Vote payload can be altered after signing but before contract processing.

actor_risk.description: Attacker modifying a legitimate vote.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering with votes corrupts the governance process; signature must cover entire payload.

assumptions: Signature on the vote message does not cover the entire, unencrypted payload being processed by the contract.
Attacker can intercept and modify the payload.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure vote messages are signed using a hash of the entire payload (proposal ID, vote choice, etc.).

threats.description: An attacker intercepts a user's vote submission and alters the vote choice (e.g., from 'Yes' to 'No') before it reaches the governance contract, provided the signature doesn't cover the change.

attack_surface.data_description: Proposal ID, vote choice, signature

security_gap.gap_description: Contract allows voting using BGT balance not controlled by the sender/signer.

actor_risk.description: Attacker votes using another user's delegated or owned BGT voting power.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Unauthorized voting undermines governance integrity; control of voting power must be strictly enforced.

assumptions: Governance contract does not correctly check that the address casting the vote controls the claimed BGT voting power (either directly or via delegation).
Attacker can submit a transaction claiming voting power they don't control.

mitigation.strategy: Governance contract must strictly verify that the voting power used for a vote belongs to the address signing/submitting the vote.

threats.description: An attacker exploits vulnerabilities to cast votes in governance proposals, leveraging the voting power of BGT owned or delegated to other users.

trace_source.matched_capability: Ensure Sufficient Voting Power, Delegate $BGT, Submit the Proposal

attack_surface.data_description: Incentive IDs, claim amount, Merkle proof, signature

security_gap.gap_description: Claim proof verification system is vulnerable to forgery.

actor_risk.description: Attacker generating fraudulent proofs to claim unearned rewards.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Weak proof verification allows claiming unearned rewards, leading to theft of assets.

assumptions: The proof generation or verification mechanism has vulnerabilities allowing forgery (e.g., Merkle proof attack, predictable proof elements).
Attacker can submit forged proofs through the Hub or directly to the distribution contract.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure the proof system (e.g., Merkle tree) is cryptographically secure and verified on-chain or via trusted oracle.

threats.description: An attacker forges a proof of entitlement to incentives and submits it to claim rewards they did not earn.

attack_surface.data_description: Incentive IDs, claim amount, Merkle proof, signature

security_gap.gap_description: Claim request details or proof payload can be altered after signing/generation but before processing.

actor_risk.description: Attacker modifying claim details in transit.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering with claims leads to incorrect distribution of valuable incentive tokens.

assumptions: The claim request or the proof itself can be modified without invalidating the signature or proof verification.
Attacker can intercept the request.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure claim requests and associated proofs are signed or integrity-protected end-to-end.

threats.description: An attacker intercepts a legitimate claim request and modifies the incentive IDs, amount, or proof to claim more or different tokens than entitled.

attack_surface.data_description: Incentive IDs, claim amount, Merkle proof, signature

security_gap.gap_description: Claim verification does not strictly tie proof to the claiming address.

actor_risk.description: Attacker claims rewards entitled to other users.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Allows theft of user rewards by submitting proofs belonging to others.

assumptions: The proof of entitlement is not correctly linked to the user's wallet address that is submitting the claim.
Attacker can obtain valid proofs for other users (e.g., API vulnerability, proof leak).

mitigation.strategy: Ensure proof generation and verification strictly link the proof to the entitled wallet address. Require claim transaction to be signed by entitled address.

threats.description: An attacker obtains or generates a valid proof for another user's incentive entitlement and uses it to claim those incentives on their own address.
